I am not super technical. heard of Make and Zapier but haven't used them that much.

Nodari Kolmakhidze

#### What's great

We’re using Trace at UniSub to clean up internal workflows, and it’s been pretty useful. What I like most is that it brings structure to messy processes without forcing you into rigid automation. You decide what’s handled by AI and what stays with the team, which matters a lot in a fast-moving startup environment.   
  
Setup was straightforward, integrations fit naturally into our stack, and it saves real time. Solid product if you care about clarity and control as you scale.

#### What needs improvement [...] ### AI Agents

Kate Ramakaieva
FuseBase

FuseBase

#### What's great

What I like most is that it’s not trying to be “100% AI.” The platform actually makes sense: it routes tasks to the right agent, whether that’s a human or AI. By connecting tools I already use like Slack, Jira, and Notion, Trace fits naturally into my existing workflows instead of forcing me to change how I work.

For repetitive content tasks, it’s a huge productivity boost - something I wouldn’t be able to replicate with pure AI alone right now.

#### What needs improvement

results.title: Trace: Fu*k The Algorithms. Build Your Own Feed. | Product Hunt
results.content:
I m curious:

2

8

Julio Jimenez

#### What's great

I'm really enjoying Trace for getting the content I care about, in the portions I care about.

I can thumbs up, or thumbs down the content I want to see more of, or the content I don't care for.

I can easily adjust how much more or less of a category I'll see in my feed.

The bookmarks allow me to create a reading list of items I want to read in the future.

Trace hits on all the features I look for.

#### What needs improvement

There are a few tiny quirks in UI rendering, quite normal for relatively new apps.

Sometimes the app suggests the free trial, even though I have already upgraded, then it catches up, and the trial component disappears.

Also some feed items are repeated at times.

Nothing major.

#### vs Alternatives [...] Nothing major.

#### vs Alternatives

Apple News

I'm not an expert in content algorithms, but I feel like if you read a certain type of content a few times, the algorithm assumes that is ALL you ever want to read, all of the time. This seems to have a compounding effect, which in my opinion is hard to break, unless you start over completely.

Tarun Tomar
Trace

Trace

Thank you so much for writing such a thorough review Julio!!! And I totally agree to the point that there are quite a few ui bugs right now but I assure you that I am working on fixing them all one by one everyday and your feedback will help me improve trace a lot. Thanks again for trying. [...] Trace

results.title: Trace: Workflow Automations for the Human 👾 AI Workforce | Product Hunt
results.content:
Nodari Kolmakhidze

#### What's great

We’re using Trace at UniSub to clean up internal workflows, and it’s been pretty useful. What I like most is that it brings structure to messy processes without forcing you into rigid automation. You decide what’s handled by AI and what stays with the team, which matters a lot in a fast-moving startup environment.   
  
Setup was straightforward, integrations fit naturally into our stack, and it saves real time. Solid product if you care about clarity and control as you scale.

#### What needs improvement [...] Kate Ramakaieva

FuseBase

#### What's great

What I like most is that it’s not trying to be “100% AI.” The platform actually makes sense: it routes tasks to the right agent, whether that’s a human or AI. By connecting tools I already use like Slack, Jira, and Notion, Trace fits naturally into my existing workflows instead of forcing me to change how I work.

For repetitive content tasks, it’s a huge productivity boost - something I wouldn’t be able to replicate with pure AI alone right now.

#### What needs improvement

The tech is strong, but more transparency and control would make it even more powerful - especially for people who want to fine-tune how work gets done rather than just trust the output.

#### vs Alternatives [...] Vlad
Trace

Trace

@timcha\_cherkasov  @rohanrecommends  @arthur\_romanov You also have to think about UX here. Too much control can bring confusion. It would be nice if the system works for a general use case out-of-the-box, and parameterisation is a feature for pro users

Andrei

@timcha\_cherkasov  @rohanrecommends You can choose to do the task yourself or assign a teammate if you believe that the task is not good enough for an AI to do. We put users in control. However, we learn from how you use the tool, and it becomes clear with time that "this user doesn't like the task of this type to be automated"

Stefan
Trace

Trace

Thanks! Trace uses the company index and workflow rules to suggest the best agent for each task, and teams can fully customize the logic to fit their processes.

results.title: Trace-AI: Know What You Ship. Secure What You Depend On. | Product Hunt
results.content:
To know more 

Lilou Lane

Wow, this is incredibly impressive! Loving how Trace-AI moves from reactive patching to proactive risk detection. Huge congrats to the team for tackling such a complex problem and making secure software shipping more achievable.

Shifa Nur

Love the metadata-first approach. Predicting risk before CVEs and no source access required fits real-world SOC workflows. The CRA alignment is a nice touch. Curious how you score maintainer behavior over time, and how noisy alerts are.

Saravanan
Gridapps Testimonials

Gridapps Testimonials

Congrats on the Launch Ram and Tem

Sriram
Trace-AI

Trace-AI

Thanks so much, @iamsaravanaa We're really excited about this launch would love to hear your thoughts if you get a chance to try it out! [...] Don't see your language or platform? Reach out directly, we actively reprioritize our roadmap based on what teams actually need. If there's demand, we'll move it up the queue.

Follow along for updates, or drop your toughest questions. Real feedback from this community is what makes products better. 🙏

Aravintharaj G
Trace-AI

Trace-AI

A while back, when I was leading engineering teams, one of the most frustrating challenges we faced wasn’t building features; it was dealing with vulnerabilities. Every time a security report came in, it completely threw off our plans and slowed everything down. [...] Alex Cloudstar
Product Hunt Wrapped 2025

Product Hunt Wrapped 2025

Love the metadata-first approach. Predicting risk before CVEs and no source access required fits real-world SOC workflows. The CRA alignment is a nice touch. Curious how you score maintainer behavior over time, and how noisy alerts are. Congrats on the launch!

Sriram
Trace-AI

Trace-AI

Thanks @alexcloudstar Really glad the metadata-first approach resonates it's exactly what we built for. Great questions on maintainer scoring and alert noise, let me break those down

We track maintainer health by analyzing three key signals over time:

Time since last commit

Commit frequency

Release cadence
